heroHeadline: Chernobyl's 40th Anniversary: IAEA Demands Sarcophagus Repairs Under Russian Threat; Ukraine Strikes Yaroslavl Refinery 1,000 km from Front
heroSubtitle: On the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster, President Zelensky accused Russia of 'nuclear terrorism' as IAEA Director General Grossi warned that damage to the nuclear sarcophagus from a 2025 Russian drone strike has compromised a key safety function and must be repaired immediately. Russian strikes continued across Ukraine: a guided aerial bomb hit a civilian minibus in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, 149 combat engagements were recorded, and Russian forces sealed a tactical pocket near Lyman. Ukraine hit back with long-range drones striking the Yaroslavl oil refinery (~1,000 km from front, processing 15M tons/year) and the PhosAgro fertilizer plant in northwest Russia for the second time in April. Moldovan President Sandu visited Kyiv amid the commemorations; the Kremlin confirmed trilateral peace talks are paused as the US-Iran conflict diverts Washington's diplomatic bandwidth.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OHCHR, ISW, CSIS, Ukrainian MoD, US DoD, NATO, IAEA, EU Council, UK MoD, and major news outlets including Reuters, BBC, AP, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Kyiv Independent, Euronews, and RBC-Ukraine. Casualty figures are estimates; Russia has never released official figures.
